title: The Basketball Court
narration: The court had cracks running through it like veins. We didn't care. We played until the streetlights came on and our moms started yelling from the balconies. Nobody kept score after a while, we just played. The backboard had no net, the lines were barely visible, and it didn't matter. I drove past it last week. The hoops are gone. Weeds are coming through the concrete. But I swear I could still hear the ball bouncing if I closed my eyes long enough.

scenes.text: The court had cracks running through it like veins. We didn't care.
